title: RESTful API Design Principles
description: REST uses standard HTTP methods and status codes. Resources are identified by URIs. Responses should be consistent and use proper content types.

title: OpenAPI Specification
description: Standard for describing RESTful APIs. Enables auto-generation of documentation, client SDKs, and server stubs. Version 3.x is current standard.

title: API Versioning Strategies
description: Managing breaking changes in APIs. Strategies include URL path versioning (/v1/), header versioning, query parameter versioning, and content negotiation.
best_practices: Use URL path versioning for simplicity

title: API Pagination
description: Techniques for returning large datasets in manageable chunks. Common approaches: offset-based, cursor-based, and keyset pagination.
best_practices: Use cursor-based pagination for large datasets

title: API Rate Limiting
description: Controlling the rate of requests clients can make to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage. Implemented using token bucket, sliding window, or fixed window algorithms.
best_practices: Provide clear error messages when limited

title: API Error Handling
description: Consistent error response format across APIs. Should include error code, message, details, and request correlation ID for debugging.
best_practices: Map internal errors to appropriate HTTP status
Never expose stack traces in production

title: API Idempotency
description: Ensuring that making the same API request multiple times has the same effect as making it once. Critical for retry-safe APIs in distributed systems.
best_practices: Use idempotency keys for POST requests
Make PUT and DELETE naturally idempotent
Return cached responses for duplicate requests

title: GraphQL Design
description: Query language for APIs that allows clients to request exactly the data they need. Provides a type system, single endpoint, and real-time subscriptions.

title: API Authentication Methods
description: Methods for authenticating API consumers: API Keys, OAuth 2.0, JWT Bearer tokens, mutual TLS. Each suited to different use cases and security requirements.
best_practices: Use short-lived access tokens with refresh

title: API Gateway Best Practices
description: API gateways serve as the single entry point for all API consumers, handling routing, authentication, rate limiting, transformation, and monitoring.
best_practices: Use circuit breakers for backend protection
